**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, SEPTEMBER 8TH**
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**
**This past week has been all about votes and it’s been a good week for Republicans! With Vincent Paul Migliore holding onto the Grafton 9 seat, winning by 50 votes—it proves that every single vote counts. Although the Democrat opponent has called for a recount, which will be held on Wednesday, September 13th, we are confident that we will hold onto the seat.**

**Did you hear? On the front page of today’s Union Leader, [“A new report on the 2016 election fund nearly 1,100 people who cast ballots here in New Hampshire were either under investigation for voting in more than one place or signed affidavits with addresses that may no longer be valid.” ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbY1qiyT3HuRDH6pVWPAnYR5OyxmcIHk6pM7MwgX5j2Vo5MEcLmE98olHck8-2BfHQladQizb3XOze-2BXXhZTkxABWUU04dEFF0y9D1nudvBpn3k3JU-2BdXdOwLwM53lhlftaf5b711ARsyJQBMr0cwY3kw4-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Mi3xT93DFWACFxlkpBDUZX9gw8oYI0EMCAC5vudcQJbvigVGCqFmDe7zd9fx5w25pwOa-2FmZHtc7qGO-2BPtIOUsckyi7ama4jdmRwEdtqyE7cGJ4a-2BJW4gZfuEDdGvxeLCOh-2BBqwmY02xgvgsD7NoUxx5PeBhX5t0jCLwAldFYVnZjfPfxhmJw3wO-2B-2BiibSHafsodhaMVddc8am4VXaT4ChjQk7RW4x7FrzABZWkM3urohDUJ8iGuEzXN6TvVDkT29kii3fnmS9eqwphv2M5fcjtg-3D-3D)**
****
**In the six years that I served as a State Senator, Republicans worked to ensure voter integrity at the ballot box. It was hard to make any gains when we had a Democrat Governor. [But this past session, with Governor Chris Sununu in the corner office, Republicans were able to pass legislation (SB3).](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbT0pW52ma3LTMrghVm-2FI6cWOrzttPYXIL8ZgL2Ta1Vr7TIyiHRPCfkSjZaeGF20Ir-2F-2BAv7kcOytxIX5Ev83s-2F7dkR6rx-2FV-2Fqy08i8Z6pDY51M3C8iwxyQffE0JhNqQKzoECGW3zjmXKe-2By6435zlnRpWp3uGgCp0csAENuTm2E-2BY_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Mi3xT93DFWACFxlkpBDUZX9gw8oYI0EMCAC5vudcQJbvigVGCqFmDe7zd9fx5w25pwOa-2FmZHtc7qGO-2BPtIOUsctqHDIsVQ7RYvBXX-2B2OQ7JV5v52MG7Om3Q6iNcF8Bz19DdA3ME9Qat2yzbDTsIRopexY-2Ft1sI03OBuDuoaymXjsxORhWk09Zu44c6cXk-2Fzidn0w4H0rsOgcjYUMGFTAe1FA9VgDUu7tIFScX1XIYTIAbkM-2FFYslfMukKdLF307kVuHnQpeg5tSADshu-2B-2B72v1Q-3D-3D)**
****
**Don’t be fooled by the Democrats claim that this new law is about voter suppression, it is not. It is about protecting your vote, and not having it diluted.**
****

**If you haven’t heard, the NH Republican State Committee will be hosting the R.E.D. Summit on Saturday, September 30th at St. Anselm College. The day-long educational and inspirational event will include lunch for only $20 per person or $10 per person for Grassroots Givers. [Be sure to visit our website and sign up today!](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=8W-2B0ovlN66MrpBErbIEL-2BdyydhTe2s9t0L44on3NwA0-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Mi3xT93DFWACFxlkpBDUZX9gw8oYI0EMCAC5vudcQJbvigVGCqFmDe7zd9fx5w25pwOa-2FmZHtc7qGO-2BPtIOUscv4MAYlFNBMv0pOsJ7SzCKoyZPGRnw44bVrdfhPluAf25WcLKUXn3oLncwtOXcRGiZ-2FVBsOZx-2F8e8flWTIL6go8xCMZwX8Uo0-2Fhulo59DFJxJcrf2ZyzY1nYFOcqmCIxdKvGxWLUhVYvYwVcJp0KzQlb0yayVPO1gOT0SsRw5gRQAsW0dHY-2FHq8sAZtoKbOe4g-3D-3D)**

**Next week there will be another Special Election in Belknap 9 with Steve Whalley. We’re looking for volunteers to help at the polls and make phone calls to get people out to vote. If you’ve got even an hour to spare, please call Joe or Tara at the office and let them know you want to help hold onto another Republican seat. I know that you could spend your valuable time on so many other things, but we know that when Republicans vote, Republicans win – [so please consider helping us get folks out to vote.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurkd4UzRFDwnUSlfHiLVwfpBRRj4XBr8rQS6IbhP1EDK7sBTZzu5rcYeHqhehxlzElg-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30Mi3xT93DFWACFxlkpBDUZX9gw8oYI0EMCAC5vudcQJbvigVGCqFmDe7zd9fx5w25pwOa-2FmZHtc7qGO-2BPtIOUscnQFy2g4zAxOPcRK3K2oK26Dh6IhvJTWGUZ5Lo7EGcVjEjjU-2BV5-2BiYDjLJw66yMcpJ6MYmyyuMgZURFC5MRz0P-2BU4HLA6tazG5Nij4PzGnt1pR938tToknLDiEcMs-2BqWJpfFFIZf8Opk-2FryEhPnvu4aovy93U0ofXIiitu2KLaKajL2NrvzdCuFhkv20mCnccw-3D-3D)**

**Jeanie**

**[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d8e6/2b07114c/18f57f9a/7314ae50/2358905703/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/protect-dreamers?source=PT-20170909-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**
Team,

Now that Donald Trump has decided to end DACA, Congress has just six months to protect Dreamers and prevent mass deportations.

Our Democratic Senators are on the case. **Senator Dick Durbin has urged his Senate colleagues to act, saying Congress has "a small and urgent window to act to avoid mass deportations."** Senator Patty Murray said this week, "we've got the votes" to fix Trump's decision -- but Mitch McConnell is standing in the way.

[**Add your name: tell Mitch McConnell to allow a vote to correct Trump 's decision on DACA and allow young Dreamers to stay in the country they call home.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d8e6/2b07114c/18f57f9a/7314ae50/2358905703/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/protect-dreamers?source=PT-20170909-Signature_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Trump's decision is a huge setback for the progress we made under President Obama, and it flies in the face of our nation's values. These young people represent the best of our country and we should not send them away.

This isn't over yet though: Congress has the chance to make this right. Our Democratic Senators are ready to ensure that young immigrants brought to the U.S. as children can continue to stay in our communities and contribute to our country. But we have to get past Mitch McConnell first.

**Sign now: tell Mitch McConnell to allow a vote on protecting Dreamers from deportation:**

name: Tulsi Gabbard, The Sanders Institute
subject: DACA
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Aloha Friend,
For so long, young people who came to this country as kids through no choice of their own, have been forced to live in the shadows, too afraid to come forward because they don't trust that our government won't come after them. Even after DACA was put in place, so many were too afraid to apply because if the program got taken away, the government would know where they live and could go after them and their families.
But 800,000 young people in this country took that leap of faith, applied for DACA, and have relied on it to obtain an education, earn a living, and establish themselves in our communities.
The current administration's decision on Tuesday to end DACA leaves them feeling betrayed and afraid after they put their trust in our government.
Last week on Maui, I sat down with some of Hawaii's DREAMers and heard their stories about living every day in fear of deportation until DACA was put into effect. They shared their stories of the opportunity and freedom they have experienced because of DACA, and the fear of uncertainty that now lies before them, with the prospects of their government targeting them and forcing them to leave the only home they've ever known.
This is not a partisan issue. It's an issue that affects communities all across this country. DACA's termination is a call for Congress to act now. We should take this opportunity to actually fix the problem once and for all and provide a permanent solution for these DREAMers, so they are not forced back into the shadows.
I have had many conversations with people about this issue. I have spoken with those that support DACA, and those that do not support it. However, the most important part of any conversation about immigration and DACA is to be informed with the facts.
**What is DACA?**
DACA is a temporary program instituted by President Barack Obama that defers immigration action and provides relief from deportation for people who were brought into the United States as children and gives them a work permit.
**Who is eligible for DACA?**
The people who apply for and receive DACA must meet a number of requirements, including: they were under the age of 16 when they were brought into the U.S., they have lived most of their lives here, they are in school or have graduated or are an honorably discharged member of the military, they have not been convicted of a felony, a significant misdemeanor, or three or more misdemeanors.
**How many people have DACA?**
There are around 800,000 recipients of DACA right now in the U.S.
**What can I do?**
At the Sanders Institute, we believe that being informed, engaged, and involved in the discussion about an issue and a policy like DACA are the first crucial steps.
[**Visit the Sanders Institute to learn more about DACA, sanctuary cities, and other immigration issues.**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7af50/bed43eb/45cd614/75ffb024/479314205/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/issues/immigration?source=em170909-full")
Thank you for staying engaged,
Tulsi Gabbard
Sanders Institute Fellow

name: Team Maggie
subject: broad-scale voter suppression
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde08/2a380d31/1cda7765/fe7919/2227085924/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde08/2a380d31/1cda7765/fe7919/2227085924/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Team --
The Trump administration is doubling down on its assault on voting rights through its so-called "Commission on Election Integrity."
Kris Kobach, the commission's leader, was dubbed the "king of voter suppression" by the ACLU.
Now, we're getting a first-hand look at why.
Kobach is laying the groundwork for broad-scale voter suppression by using deceiving and irrelevant data to rehash the same false claims that have been debunked time and again by independent analysis and members of both parties in New Hampshire.
The law is clear that college students and other New Hampshire voters can vote without getting a New Hampshire ID. **These false partisan claims are deliberately twisting the facts to baselessly suggest that Senator Hassan won through voter fraud.**
These attacks aren't just un-American and politically motivated -- they're also an affront to the dedication and hard work you put in to help elect Senator Hassan.
We need your help to correct the record, everyone. [**Sign the petition to tell Kris Kobach, Donald Trump, and far-right Republicans to stop attacking voting rights.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde08/2a380d31/1cda7765/fe791a/2227085924/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170909_VotingRights_act?source=e20170909_votingrights_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264%")
The commission is pulling some pretty ugly tricks, everyone. _It even requested private voter information, including social security data, all to silence voters and keep people away from the polls._
This is oppressive, and this is unacceptable. That's why it's imperative that we all speak out against these attacks on voting rights.
